headline: SAGA METALS SNAPS UP Labrador Heavy Rare Earth Project — Peak Values 2.03% TREO With Strange Lake-Grade Potential
snippet: SAGA Metals has signed a definitive share purchase agreement to acquire the Wolverine heavy rare earth project in central Labrador — 230.5 km² of royalty-free ground with peak TREO of 2.03% and HREO enrichment of 24–28%, comparable to Strange Lake and Tanbreez. A 2026 drill program targeting a maiden NI 43-101 resource estimate is already planned.

headline: SILVER HITS $77: Sixth Straight Supply Deficit Collides With AI Grid Demand in Historic 2026 Squeeze
snippet: Silver reached $77.40/oz this morning as structural supply deficits — now in their sixth consecutive year — collide with voracious industrial demand from AI data centre power grids and renewable energy infrastructure. Pan American Silver and Coeur Mining led mining equity gainers; analysts are debating whether silver hits triple digits before 2027.

headline: BONANZA GRADE: West Red Lake Gold Drills 215 g/t Au Over 5.35m at Madsen Mine's New Austin 904 Complex
snippet: West Red Lake Gold (TSX-V: WRLG) delivered blockbuster results from the newly accessed Austin 904 Complex at the Madsen Mine, Red Lake district: 215.46 g/t Au over 5.35m, including 388.9 g/t over 2.95m, plus additional intercepts of 50.34 g/t over 4.05m and 61.70 g/t over 3m. Initial production from the 904 Complex is targeted for H1 2027.

headline: AbraSilver Punches 171 Metres of 0.83 g/t Au at Diablillos as Phase VI Drilling Opens New Ground in Argentina
snippet: AbraSilver Resource Corp. (TSX: ABRA) released Phase VI step-out drill results at Oculto East, Diablillos, Argentina: 171.0m grading 0.83 g/t Au and 10.3 g/t Ag from 230m depth — extending the resource boundary ahead of a definitive feasibility study update in H1 2026. Higher-grade intervals within the broad zone support the geological model.

headline: COPPER CRACKS $12,000: AI Build-Out, Pentagon Demand, and 50% Section 232 Tariffs Create Triple Demand Shock
snippet: Copper surged past $12,000/t on the LME as three simultaneous demand shocks hit a market already strained by tight mine supply: AI data centre construction, accelerating defence procurement, and President Trump's April 6 enforcement of 50% Section 232 copper tariffs. Analysts warn continued volatility through at least the June 30 refined-copper tariff review deadline.

headline: DENISON READY TO BUILD: Phoenix ISR Uranium Mine Declares Construction-Ready as DNN Stock Jumps 5%
snippet: Denison Mines (NYSE: DNN) declared its Wheeler River Phoenix in-situ recovery uranium mine construction-ready and provided a capital cost update, sending shares up 5.06% on April 13. Phoenix — set to become Canada's first ISR uranium mine — broke ground at the Athabasca Basin site this spring and targets first production by mid-2028.

headline: RARE EARTHS ENTER HYPERGROWTH: Surging EV, Defense, and AI Demand Reshapes the Critical Minerals Landscape
snippet: A new market report released today declares rare earth elements have entered a structural hypergrowth phase driven by EV motor magnets, defense systems, and AI hardware — sectors where substitution is effectively impossible. The report highlights a widening demand-supply gap through 2030 as Western nations race to build ex-China supply chains.

headline: RECORD HIGHS: Gold and Silver Surge as Islamabad Peace Talks Collapse Oil Prices and Revive Safe-Haven Bids
snippet: Gold and silver hit fresh record highs today as Islamabad-brokered geopolitical negotiations triggered a collapse in crude oil and a simultaneous rush into precious metals. The move reinforces growing investor conviction that geopolitical uncertainty — not just monetary policy — is the dominant structural driver of the current metals bull market.

headline: OPINION: Gold's $4,700+ Surge Isn't About Iran — It's a Referendum on Fiscal and Monetary Stability
snippet: A market commentary argues gold's April 2026 surge above $4,700 signals deep investor anxiety about fiscal deficits and central bank credibility — not merely geopolitical risk. The author points to aggressive central bank reserve diversification away from the US dollar and persistent energy-driven inflation expectations as the real structural drivers.

headline: ASIAN METALS OPEN: Gold Holds Above $4,750 as China Physical Buying Offsets Overnight Geopolitical Jitters
snippet: GoldSeek's April 14 Asian metals market update tracks gold holding above $4,750/oz in early trading, supported by steady Chinese physical demand that is providing a price floor despite renewed geopolitical uncertainty. Silver and copper showed mixed performance as traders weigh competing macro signals heading into the North American session.
